questions.text: I tend to run on my own internal clock rather than watching the time closely.
I find that my emotional responses don't produce noticeable physical symptoms.
I notice that physical sensations rarely accompany my emotional states.
I believe that emotional intensity is one of my defining characteristics.
I prefer environments where norms and rules are stated outright.
I pick up on the subtle ways people signal agreement or dissent without speaking.
I feel that it is healthy and right to put my own needs first in most situations.
I often forgo recognition so that others can be acknowledged instead.
I am drawn to roles where I support and enable others' success.
I tend to miss it when someone is signaling that they want to be included.
I hold the view that it is perfectly acceptable to choose personal benefit over collective benefit.
I prefer to enter conversations without a script so I can respond authentically.
I feel that yielding space to others is a natural extension of caring about the group.
I feel a strong pull to finish what I start, even when enthusiasm fades.
I am inclined to make a choice and commit rather than keep options open.
I hold the view that emotional detachment in the face of suffering is a strategic advantage that benefits the very people I am trying to help.
I believe that a society's moral health can be measured by how seriously it takes the suffering of its most vulnerable members.
I hold the view that structured, reasoned engagement with suffering is more sustainable and more effective than emotionally driven engagement.
